---
title: "easyNRD Specification"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{easyNRD Specification}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

# Purpose

This document is the implementation contract for the alpha rewrite of
`easyNRD`. It defines what the package does, what it does not do, and which
behaviors are protected by tests. It is a specification, not a tutorial.

The package exists to support correct, readable, DuckDB-backed analysis of the
AHRQ Nationwide Readmissions Database (NRD) for clinical research.

The package supports NRD data years 2016 and later only.

- 2016 is the first NRD year with a full calendar year of ICD-10-CM/PCS data.
- The `I10_DX*` and `I10_PR*` column naming convention used throughout
  `easyNRD` starts with that ICD-10 transition.
- The 2015 NRD is a split file with nine months of ICD-9-CM and three months
  of ICD-10-CM/PCS and uses a different file structure.
- Data prior to 2016 uses ICD-9-CM and a different column naming scheme
  entirely.

# Governing Principles

The rewrite follows these priorities, in order:

1. Correctness above all else.
2. Clarity for human developers.
3. Deliberate DuckDB efficiency.
4. Minimal alpha scope.
5. CRAN discipline.

When principles conflict, higher items win.

# Unit of Analysis

`easyNRD` is a discharge-level package. Each input row represents one discharge
record exactly as delivered in the NRD.

The package does not reconstruct transfer chains, re-collapse same-day stays,
or manufacture a higher-level episode representation. HCUP has already applied
 its own preprocessing before release of the NRD, and the raw transfer pairs are
not available in the delivered data.

# Identifier Semantics

The implementation must respect the following NRD identifier rules:

- `KEY_NRD` identifies one discharge record.
- `NRD_VISITLINK` links discharges for a patient within the delivered linkage
  scope.
- `HOSP_NRD` identifies hospitals within a year.
- `NRD_STRATUM` identifies survey strata within a year.
- `YEAR` must scope every linkage operation.

Implications:

- `KEY_NRD` is never a patient identifier.
- `NRD_VISITLINK` cannot be used across years.
- `HOSP_NRD` cannot be used to track hospitals across years.
- `NRD_STRATUM` cannot be pooled as if strata labels were comparable across
  years.
- Any patient-level join that ignores `YEAR` is incorrect.

# Multi-year Pooling Contract

Multi-year NRD pooling is common, but the package must enforce the correct
methodology.

- `NRD_VISITLINK`, `HOSP_NRD`, and `NRD_STRATUM` are all year-specific.
- Readmission linkage must always run within one year.
- Survey design is valid only within one year.
- The package makes no attempt to deduplicate or re-key `KEY_NRD` in pooled
  outputs.
- `YEAR` is always required to disambiguate records in a pooled table.

The only supported pooling strategy is:

1. ingest one year at a time
2. run the full single-year pipeline within that year
3. export the yearly result
4. union the per-year rows after yearly processing is complete

Pooling is never a join of raw multi-year NRD data.

# Time Semantics

The implementation uses HCUP timing definitions directly.

- `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT` is the admission day on a per-patient timeline.
- `LOS` is length of stay.
- `Discharge_Day = NRD_DAYSTOEVENT + LOS`.
- The exact gap between two discharges for the same patient-year is:

```r
gap = NRD_DAYSTOEVENT_next - NRD_DAYSTOEVENT_prev - LOS_prev
```

This formula is the only correct readmission-gap formula used by the package.

# Year-End Censoring

The alpha package supports one censoring rule only: HCUP-compliant month-based
censoring using `DMONTH`.

For a readmission window `w` in days, an index discharge is eligible only when:

```r
DMONTH <= 12L - ceiling(w / 30L)
```

Examples:

- 30-day window: `DMONTH <= 11`
- 90-day window: `DMONTH <= 9`

There are no alternative censoring modes, no day-of-year censoring logic, no
leap-year handling branch, and no user option to relax censoring.

# Public API

The alpha version exports exactly these functions and nothing else:

```r
nrd_ingest(paths)
nrd_pipeline(paths, .f, output_dir = NULL)
nrd_prepare(data)
nrd_flag_condition(data, name, pattern = NULL, codes = NULL, type, scope)
nrd_link_readmissions(data, index_condition, readmit_condition, window = 30L, readmit_vars = NULL)
nrd_select(data, ...)
nrd_export(data, path)
nrd_as_survey(data)
nrd_label(data, ..., .keep_original = FALSE)
nrd_factorize(data)
nrd_demographics()
nrd_hospital_vars()
nrd_close(data)
nrd_cache_dir()
nrd_cleanup(path, force = FALSE)
```

No deleted function remains as an exported compatibility shim.

# Explicit Non-Goals

The alpha rewrite does not provide:

- transfer or same-day stay re-collapsing
- internal Elixhauser or CCSR grouping based on toy crosswalks
- a public pivot helper
- alternative censoring methods
- optional censored-follow-up behavior
- public DuckDB configuration knobs
- public connection-inspection helpers
- a materialization wrapper beyond export and re-ingest composition

# DuckDB Execution Model

The package is designed around DuckDB-backed lazy execution.

- Public pipeline functions operate lazily unless a deliberate eager pass is
  required by the specification.
- `nrd_ingest()` opens a fresh in-memory DuckDB connection per call.
- Every new connection configures a process-scoped `temp_directory` under
  `nrd_cache_dir()`.
- Every new connection sets `preserve_insertion_order = FALSE`.
- The package never uses `read.csv()` or `DBI::dbWriteTable()` to ingest NRD
  files.
- The package never uses `PIVOT`, `string_agg()`, or `list()` aggregates in SQL
  generation.
- Expensive joins should project and filter early.
- `dplyr::compute()` checkpoints are used deliberately inside DuckDB before and
  between blocking readmission-linkage stages to reduce intermediate plan
  width and break up stacked window operators.

# Function Contracts

## `nrd_ingest(paths)`

`nrd_ingest()` accepts one of three inputs:

- a character vector of `.parquet` file paths
- an Arrow dataset or Arrow query
- an existing DuckDB-backed lazy table, returned unchanged

Behavioral contract:

- Opens a fresh in-memory DuckDB connection for parquet-path or Arrow inputs.
- Configures `temp_directory` to a new process-scoped subdirectory under
  `nrd_cache_dir()` with the form `easyNRD_<pid>_<token>`.
- Sets `preserve_insertion_order = FALSE` silently.
- Registers parquet data through `arrow::open_dataset()` and
  `arrow::to_duckdb()`.
- Standardizes exactly these column-name variants:
  - `NRD_VisitLink` -> `NRD_VISITLINK`
  - `NRD_DaysToEvent` -> `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT`
- Attaches owner metadata so `nrd_close()` can release resources.
- Returns a DuckDB-backed `tbl_lazy`.
- Never calls `collect()`.

`nrd_ingest()` accepts one or more parquet files representing compatible NRD
data years 2016 and later. It does not normalize older ICD-9-era file layouts.

## `nrd_pipeline(paths, .f, output_dir = NULL)`

`nrd_pipeline()` is the required public wrapper for methodology-safe multi-year
analysis.

Behavioral contract:

- `paths` must be a non-empty named character vector or named list of parquet
  paths.
- Names must be non-missing, non-empty, and unique, and are processed in the
  supplied order.
- `.f` must be a function.
- For each named year label, in order:
  1. call `nrd_ingest()` on that year's parquet path
  2. call `.f(data)` on the ingested single-year table
  3. assert that `.f` returned a DuckDB-backed lazy table and did not collect
  4. export the yearly result to `NRD_<year>_pipeline.parquet`
  5. close the yearly ingest resources with `nrd_close()`
  6. emit a message that includes the year label and elapsed time
- If `.f()` or export fails for a given year, the function aborts with that year
  label in the error and preserves the original parent error.
- The implementation never passes raw parquet paths from multiple years into one
  pre-processing `nrd_ingest()` call.
- After all yearly exports succeed, the function calls `nrd_ingest()` on the
  exported yearly parquet files and returns the pooled DuckDB-backed lazy table
  invisibly.
- When `output_dir = NULL`, the function uses a temporary intermediate parquet
  directory, deletes it on error, and registers it for deletion when
  `nrd_close()` is called on the pooled object.
- After `nrd_close()` is called on the pooled object, the temporary
  intermediate parquet files are gone and the table must not be queried again.
- DuckDB session temp directories remain separate from pipeline intermediate
  parquet directories and are managed by the existing owner mechanism.
- Never calls `collect()`.

## `nrd_prepare(data)`

`nrd_prepare()` accepts a DuckDB-backed lazy table from `nrd_ingest()`.

Behavioral contract:

- Asserts required columns:
  `NRD_VISITLINK`, `KEY_NRD`, `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT`, `LOS`, `DMONTH`, `DIED`,
  `YEAR`.
- Adds one column only:

```r
Discharge_Day = NRD_DAYSTOEVENT + LOS
```

- Does not modify existing columns.
- Does not drop or add rows.
- Does not re-collapse transfers.
- Never calls `collect()`.

## `nrd_flag_condition(data, name, pattern = NULL, codes = NULL, type, scope)`

`nrd_flag_condition()` creates a logical phenotype flag from diagnosis or
procedure columns.

Behavioral contract:

- Exactly one of `pattern` or `codes` must be supplied.
- `type` is either:
  - `"dx"` for `I10_DX1` through `I10_DX40`
  - `"pr"` for `I10_PR1` through `I10_PR25`
- `scope` is one of:
  - `"principal"` for the first column only
  - `"secondary"` for positions 2 and higher
  - `"all"` for all available columns in the type
- Pattern mode applies regex directly to data values and must translate to SQL
  regex on DuckDB.
- Codes mode performs exact matching with SQL `IN (...)` semantics.
- `NA` values must evaluate to `FALSE` in the final flag.
- No internal static ICD dictionary is consulted.
- Never calls `collect()`.

## `nrd_link_readmissions(data, index_condition, readmit_condition, window = 30L, readmit_vars = NULL)`

`nrd_link_readmissions()` links the first qualifying readmission back to each
eligible index discharge while preserving the full denominator.

Required columns:

- `YEAR`
- `NRD_VISITLINK`
- `KEY_NRD`
- `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT`
- `LOS`
- `DMONTH`
- `DIED`

Required algorithm:

1. Compute index eligibility:

```r
is_index_eligible = DMONTH <= 12L - ceiling(window / 30L)
```

2. Compute index status:

```r
IndexEvent = is_index_eligible & DIED == 0L & (!!index_condition)
```

3. Materialize a narrowed linkage checkpoint with `dplyr::compute()` inside
   DuckDB before the self-join.
4. Create an index pool containing only index rows and only the columns needed
   for linkage.
5. Create a candidate pool containing rows satisfying `readmit_condition`, with
   no censoring filter, and only the columns needed for linkage plus requested
   `readmit_vars`.
6. Join candidate and index pools on `YEAR` and `NRD_VISITLINK`.
7. Exclude self-matches with `KEY_NRD_cand != KEY_NRD_idx`.
8. Compute the gap exactly as:

```r
gap = DTE_cand - DTE_idx - LOS_idx
```

9. Keep only `gap >= 1L & gap <= window`.
10. For each index discharge, keep the first qualifying readmission using:
    - smallest `gap`
    - then smallest `DTE_cand`
    - then smallest `KEY_NRD_cand`
11. Materialize sequential checkpoint tables between the `gap` ranking pass and
    the `DTE_cand` ranking pass so each blocking window stage runs in its own
    DuckDB query plan.
12. Left join the first-readmission map back to the full annotated denominator
    table.

Output contract:

- All input rows are preserved.
- `IndexEvent` is stored as integer `1L` or `0L`.
- Added columns include:
  - `IndexEvent`
  - `time_to_event`
  - `outcome_status`
  - `readmit_*` columns derived from `readmit_vars`
- `outcome_status` is one of:
  - `"Readmitted"`
  - `"Censored"`
  - `"Died at Index"`
  - `NA`
- `time_to_event` is:
  - exact `gap` for readmitted index rows
  - `0` for died-at-index rows
  - capped remaining window days for censored index rows
  - `NA` for non-index rows
- If `Discharge_Day` exists from `nrd_prepare()`, it may be used in censored
  follow-up calculations; otherwise the same quantity is computed internally as
  `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT + LOS`.
- The function never calls `collect()`.

## `nrd_select(data, ...)`

`nrd_select()` is a projection helper that preserves essential identifiers and
analysis fields.

Behavioral contract:

- It errors if any of these linkage-critical identifiers are missing:
  - `YEAR`
  - `NRD_VISITLINK`
  - `KEY_NRD`
- It always retains those three columns.
- It also retains these columns if present:
  - `HOSP_NRD`
  - `DISCWT`
  - `NRD_STRATUM`
  - `IndexEvent`
  - `time_to_event`
  - `outcome_status`
  - `Discharge_Day`
  - `DMONTH`
  - `DIED`
  - `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT`
  - `LOS`
- Optional retained columns are selected with `dplyr::any_of()` semantics so
  the function can be used before survey variables are present.
- User-selected columns supplied through `...` are appended.
- The function works on both lazy tables and in-memory data frames.

## `nrd_export(data, path)`

Behavioral contract:

- `path` must end in `.parquet`.
- Parent directories are created if needed.
- For DuckDB-backed lazy tables, export uses rendered SQL and DuckDB `COPY`.
- Export settings are fixed to:

```sql
FORMAT PARQUET, COMPRESSION ZSTD, ROW_GROUP_SIZE 122880
```

- The row-group size is not user-configurable in the alpha.
- Returns `path` invisibly.

## `nrd_as_survey(data)`

Behavioral contract:

- Asserts `HOSP_NRD`, `DISCWT`, and `NRD_STRATUM`.
- Computes survey degrees of freedom as:

```r
n_distinct(HOSP_NRD) - n_distinct(NRD_STRATUM)
```

- This degree-of-freedom calculation is one deliberate eager database round
  trip and should be documented as such in code.
- Returns:

```r
srvyr::as_survey(
  data,
  id = HOSP_NRD,
  weights = DISCWT,
  strata = NRD_STRATUM,
  nest = TRUE,
  degf = degf
)
```

- Works on in-memory data frames and lazy DuckDB tables.
- Valid only for a single NRD year.
- Must not be called on pooled output from `nrd_pipeline()`.

Correct weighted multi-year workflow:

1. process each year independently
2. create the survey design within that year with `nrd_as_survey()`
3. compute year-specific weighted estimates
4. combine the estimates after the per-year survey analysis step

## `nrd_label(data, ..., .keep_original = FALSE)`

`nrd_label()` remains in scope because its current behavior is substantively
correct.

Behavioral contract:

- Works on lazy tables and in-memory data frames.
- Applies labels from `nrd_dict`.
- Pushes `CASE WHEN` logic to SQL for lazy tables.
- Supports `.keep_original = TRUE` by preserving original coded columns and
  writing labeled values to suffixed columns.

## `nrd_factorize(data)`

`nrd_factorize()` also remains in scope with light cleanup only.

Behavioral contract:

- Works on in-memory data frames and survey objects backed by in-memory data.
- Errors clearly on lazy input.
- Converts labeled columns to factors using `nrd_dict` level order.

## `nrd_close(data)`

Behavioral contract:

- Retrieves owner metadata from a managed DuckDB-backed lazy table.
- Disconnects the underlying connection with:

```r
DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)
```

- Removes the associated session temp directory.
- Removes any registered extra cleanup paths such as temporary intermediate
  parquet directories created by `nrd_pipeline(output_dir = NULL)`.
- Marks the owner as closed.
- Returns `TRUE` invisibly if resources were released.
- Returns `FALSE` invisibly if the object was already closed or unmanaged.
- After `nrd_close()` succeeds, the managed table must not be queried again.

The alpha implementation uses a thin owner wrapper only. It does not rely on
`reg.finalizer()`.

## `nrd_cache_dir()`

Behavioral contract:

- Resolves the cache directory in this order:
  1. `EASYNRD_CACHE_DIR`
  2. `getOption("easynrd.cache_dir")`
  3. `tools::R_user_dir("easyNRD", "cache")`
- Creates the directory if needed.

## `nrd_cleanup(path, force = FALSE)`

Behavioral contract:

- Removes only `easyNRD_*` subdirectories under `path`.
- Uses PID-aware liveness checks to avoid deleting session directories owned by
  active processes when `force = FALSE`.
- Removes all matching directories when `force = TRUE`.

# Required Test Invariants

The following invariants are part of the package contract and must be protected
by tests.

1. Denominator preservation: `nrow(nrd_link_readmissions(output))` equals the
   input row count.
2. Year scope: readmission linkage never crosses `YEAR`.
3. Gap formula correctness: readmitted `time_to_event` equals exactly
   `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT_readmit - NRD_DAYSTOEVENT_index - LOS_index`.
4. First-only linkage: only the smallest-gap readmission is linked; ties break
   by smallest `NRD_DAYSTOEVENT_cand`, then smallest `KEY_NRD_cand`.
5. Index censoring: no row with
   `DMONTH > 12 - ceiling(window / 30)` has `IndexEvent == 1L`.
6. Death exclusion: `DIED == 1` at the index stay produces
   `outcome_status == "Died at Index"`, and dead patients are never index
   events.
7. Regex correctness: diagnosis and procedure regex matching is applied to the
   actual data columns, not to a truncated static code list.
8. Survey domain correctness: `srvyr::filter()` domain analysis on the survey
   object matches `survey::subset()` on the equivalent in-memory design.
9. Multi-year orchestration: `nrd_pipeline()` processes one year at a time,
   exports yearly results, and pools only by re-ingesting the per-year exports.
10. Pipeline return type: `.f` returning a collected or Arrow-backed object
    aborts clearly because the pipeline result must stay DuckDB-backed and lazy.
11. Pipeline cleanup: temporary intermediate parquet files created by
    `nrd_pipeline(output_dir = NULL)` are deleted on error and by `nrd_close()`
    on the pooled result.
12. Lazy execution: `nrd_prepare()`, `nrd_flag_condition()`, and `nrd_select()`
    never call `collect()`.
13. Readmission checkpointing: `nrd_link_readmissions()` uses multiple
    deliberate internal `dplyr::compute()` checkpoints but never calls
    `collect()`.
14. SQL snapshot stability: snapshot tests cover `nrd_flag_condition()` in both
    pattern and codes modes and cover `nrd_link_readmissions()` SQL rendering.
15. SQL mode isolation: pattern-mode SQL must not contain `IN (...)`, and
    codes-mode SQL must not contain regex SQL such as `regexp_matches`.

# Test Fixture Requirements

The synthetic test helper must exercise the real ingest path.

- `make_synthetic_nrd()` writes a parquet file with
  `arrow::write_parquet()`.
- The helper returns a file path, not an in-memory tibble.
- Tests clean up with `on.exit(unlink(path), add = TRUE)`.

This ensures ingest tests exercise real Arrow-to-DuckDB registration, fresh
connection setup, temp-directory configuration, and insertion-order settings.

# File and Surface Discipline

The alpha rewrite keeps only the minimal file surface needed to support the
specified API and tests.

- `sysdata.rda` contains only `nrd_dict`.
- `globals.R` lists only names referenced by the surviving code.
- Every public function has a complete roxygen block with examples.
- Every internal helper uses the `.nrd_` prefix and has a short explanatory
  comment.
- No public pipeline function calls `collect()` except the intentional eager
  pass inside `nrd_as_survey()` for degree-of-freedom calculation.

# Success Criteria

The rewrite is complete only when the implementation matches this contract,
deleted surfaces are gone, tests protect the invariants above, and the package
passes `R CMD check` with zero errors, zero warnings, and zero notes other than
the accepted global-variable pattern handled through `globals.R`.
